description: Avoid longs during London/NY session. The weakest filter discovered shows hour_cos < 0.63 AND direction >= 0.50 (London/NY longs) at only 8.3% win rate â€” half the baseline (0.50x lift, n=12). Combined with the strong hour_cos Ã— direction interaction (0.1227 SHAP), this suggests a structural session-direction bias: gold tends to sell off during London/NY in this sample window, making long entries during 03:30-20:30 UTC systematically unfavorable. This can be implemented by adding a direction-aware session filter as a new edge, or as a first approximation, by narrowing the allowed trading window to the overlap where both directions work (roughly 18:00-03:30 UTC for longs, keeping London/NY open for shorts only). However, since no existing config param directly encodes directionÃ—session, the most practical proxy is to raise the confluence bar specifically for the permissive direction.
expected_improvement: By requiring score >= 4 (at least 2 structural alignment components), we indirectly filter out the low-quality directional trades that lack multi-timeframe confirmation. The 12 long trades during London/NY with 8.3% win rate likely have low confluence. This is a blunt proxy â€” a proper directionÃ—session filter would be more surgical, but this is testable immediately with existing config. Expected to cut trade count by ~30-40% while lifting win rate by 3-5pp.
